page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-224 is a compute-focused virtual machine powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 224 dedicated vCPUs derived from 112 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, utilizing 2.0 threads per core. The system is equipped with 224.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests ranking in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is also in the top tier, while large-block read and write bandwidths remain strong in the top 25%. In single-core operations, the instance registers strong to average performance, with stress-ng single-core in the top 25% and Geekbench single-core in the middle 50%. Cryptographic and physics simulations perform exceptionally well, as evidenced by top-tier PassMark CPU encryption and physics scores.

The hardware profile presents clear resource tradeoffs. While the high vCPU count delivers massive parallel processing capabilities, the low memory allocation of 1.0 GB per vCPU limits its utility for memory-intensive datasets. This resource density is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints, avoiding the premium costs associated with unused RAM. Additionally, the lack of local storage requires external storage attachment, and LLM text generation for large 70B models is weak, falling into the bottom 25%.

This server type is designed for highly parallel, CPU-bound tasks. Suitable workloads include software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, static web serving, and high-throughput database operations, such as Redis SET operations which achieved top-tier benchmark results.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-224 is an x86_64 compute-focused server featuring 224 dedicated vCPUs from 112 physical AMD EPYC 7B13 cores and 224.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it provides a lean 1.0 GB of memory per vCPU. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, PassMark encryption, and Redis SET operations, alongside strong cached memory bandwidth. However, single-core performance and large-model LLM text generation are average to weak. This resource configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for highly parallel, CPU-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints, avoiding the expense of unneeded RAM. It is best suited for software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and parallel database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-224 features 224 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 224 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier multi-core performance and is optimized for parallel, CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-224 is a 224-vCPU AMD EPYC server with 224 GB RAM, offering top-tier multi-core CPU performance.
tagline: A high-density, compute-focused server offering 224 dedicated vCPUs for parallel processing and high-throughput cryptographic workloads without memory overhead.
bullet_points: 224 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors
Top-tier multi-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, optimized for compute-heavy workloads
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators
Suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving
